one_line_takeaway: A three-player (generator/discriminator/classifier) conditional GAN with two discriminator-stabilization terms generates balanced, class-distinguishable defect images that improve imbalanced anomaly-detection classifiers in additive manufacturing — a manufacturing ML paper, not a security paper.
key_finding: Across an ablation and four datasets the proposed method beats no-augmentation, oversampling, and prior GAN baselines under imbalance. In the MNIST-fashion ablation (balanced ratio 0.10) it raised F-score from 0.783 (baseline) to 0.830 and cut FID sharply (e.g., T-shirt 203.9 -> 109.3 vs. plain conditional GAN). The two stability terms (Variant 3 vs Variant 2) drove the largest FID gains, and a gradient-norm plot shows the proposed generator gradient stays small/low-variance while the un-stabilized variant explodes. On real AM data the method improved F-score by 3-13% (polymer FFF) and 4-15% (metal EBM) over benchmarks; notably it also beat Cooperative GAN, isolating the value of the added discriminator stabilization.
core_contribution: A GAN-based data-augmentation method for supervised anomaly/defect detection in additive manufacturing (AM) under imbalanced sensor-image data. The method uses a three-player architecture (discriminator, generator, classifier) that is jointly and alternately optimized: the generator+discriminator perform adversarial learning to produce realistic minority-class (defect) images, while the generator+classifier cooperate (via a shared cross-entropy term) to make generated samples state-distinguishable. The paper's OWN novel delta over the closest cited three-player method (Choi et al. 2021, 'Cooperative GAN') is two added stabilization terms in the discriminator objective: (1) a gradient penalty enforcing 1-Lipschitz continuity, and (2) an auxiliary 'mislabeled-sample' task where the discriminator must treat an actual image paired with a wrong state label as fake. Both terms prevent generator-gradient explosion and improve generated-sample quality/diversity.
threat_model: Not applicable. This is a manufacturing quality-assurance / machine-learning paper, not a security paper. The word 'adversarial' refers to the GAN generator-vs-discriminator minimax game, not to an attacker. There is no adversary, no attack surface, and no threat model; the knowledge-graph cluster label 'GAN Attacks & Forgery' is a misclassification.
limitations: Gains on the open-source comparison are modest (MNIST-fashion F-score 0.642 proposed vs 0.634 BAGAN-GP vs 0.621 baseline; CIFAR-10 0.652 vs 0.617 baseline). On the metal EBM process, GAN-based methods (including intermediate variants) often underperformed even the no-augmentation baseline because actual images were few and highly overlapped, giving the generator too little to learn — the proposed method still won but the whole GAN paradigm was fragile there. Only three narrow, visually-similar class subsets are used per dataset; balanced ratios that were 'too low to be meaningful' were excluded, so very-extreme imbalance is not characterized. No statistical-significance testing beyond 10-run averages, and improvements are reported as ranges over benchmarks rather than per-baseline deltas in the AM studies (figures, not tables).

datasets_used: MNIST-fashion (ablation: T-shirt/Pullover/Dress, balanced ratio 0.10, 800 majority / 80 minority samples; open-source study: 5 upper-clothing labels)

Polymer AM Fused Filament Fabrication (FFF) surface images: Hyrel System 30M printer, ABS filament; normal vs under-fill (feed-rate) vs under-fill (cooling-fan); 915 normal, 591 under-feed, 459 under-fan samples; ROI 460x213x3; balanced ratios 0.10/0.15/0.20

Metal AM Electron Beam Melting (EBM) surface images: Arcam Q10plus, Ti-6Al-4V; classify scan strategy Raster (majority) vs Dehoff/Random (minority); 322 images/strategy, 120x120 patches; balanced ratios 0.3/0.4/0.5
quantitative_results: Ablation MNIST-fashion (ratio 0.10): F-score Baseline 0.783 -> Variant1 0.791 -> Variant2 0.806 -> Proposed 0.830; Precision 0.785 -> 0.814; Recall 0.873 -> 0.900
Ablation FID (lower=better), Proposed vs Variant1: T-shirt 109.3 vs 203.9; Pullover 142.9 vs 239.9; Dress 184.3 vs 226.1
Open-source MNIST-fashion F-score: Proposed 0.642 (best) vs BAGAN-GP 0.634, CDRAGAN 0.632, Baseline 0.621, SMOTE 0.612, Cooperative GAN 0.619; Precision 0.664; Recall 0.713
Open-source CIFAR-10 F-score: Proposed 0.652 (best) vs B-SMOTE 0.642, BAGAN-GP 0.634, Baseline 0.617; Recall 0.684
Polymer FFF: Proposed improved F-score 3-13%, Precision 3-10%, Recall 2-10% over benchmarks across ratios 0.10-0.20
Metal EBM: Proposed improved F-score 4-15%, Precision 2-8%, Recall 3-8% over benchmarks across ratios 0.3-0.5
baselines_compared: Cooperative GAN (Choi et al. 2021) — closest prior three-player method
